_what: A transparent, hand-specified fingerprint of the classic beaten-down-then-explodes setup (the MU-2016 / INTC / DELL-before-the-run pattern): a quality name that fell hard, stopped falling, coiled on dried-up volume after a capitulation washout, and is starting to turn. Price/volume only (Stage A). Stage B adds the fundamental floor (analyst upside, rising EPS, profitability) that separates a real turnaround from a value trap.
_honesty: This is NOT a backtested edge and NOT a fitted ML model. It is a heuristic scorecard whose feature ranges were chosen to echo a handful of historical blow-up setups. Treat the score as a way to RANK beaten-down names for human research, not as a signal to trade.

dd_from_high.desc: drawdown from the trailing 1y high — beaten down but not annihilated (sweet spot ~-45%)

base_position.desc: position within the 52w range (0=low,1=high); low = sitting near the base

downtrend_stall.desc: the freefall has stalled — 1m return no longer worse than the average daily drift of the 6m slide

vol_compression.desc: 21d realized vol below its 1y median — the coil / volatility contraction before a move

capitulation_vol.desc: a recent heavy-volume washout (max 5d-avg volume / 1y avg) — seller exhaustion

early_turn.desc: the kick — short-term momentum turning up relative to the trailing trend
